revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chausuyama Kofun (Ōtsu)]][[ja:茶臼山古墳 (大津市)]][[zh:茶臼山古墳 (大津市)]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Chausuyama Kofun | native_name = 茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Chausuyama Kofun (Otsu), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.990325|N|135.884014|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Chausuyama Kofun'''|茶臼山古墳|||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Akibadai neighborhood of [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1553 |title=茶臼山古墳・小茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Chausuyama Kofun / Kochausuyama |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> With a total length of 122 meters, it is the third largest ''kofun'' in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Chausuyama Kofun is located on a hill on the southern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|qid=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]'|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the east. It was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the shards of cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The location and construction of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remains unknown as it has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. From its construction technique and ''haniwa'', the tumulus is estimated to have been built from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century AD, or the middleof the Kofun period. It is the largest of a cluster of tumuli in the surrounding area, including the {{nihongo|'''Kochausuyama Kofun'''|小茶臼山古墳|||qq=}}, an 18-meter diameter {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}) which is included in the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> There is a long tradition connecting this ''kofun'' to the family of [[:en:Prince Ōtomo|Prince Ōtomo]] (c.648 - 672 AD), the son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, who committed suicide after his defeat by his uncle [[:en:Prince Ōama|Prince Ōama]] in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is located about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Zezehommachi Station|en|Zezehommachi Station|qid=Q8069983|lt=Zezehommachi Station|1=Zezehommachi Station}} on the {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|qid=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Overall length: 122 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 8 meter high ; "Neck" portion: 56 meters wide ;Anterior rectangular portion: 58 meters wide x 60 meters long ==Gallery== <gallery> File:Chausuyama Kofun (Otsu), kouenbu.jpg|posterior portion File:Chausuyama Kofun (Otsu), Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine located on the saddle of the tumulus File:Chausuyama Kofun (Otsu), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior portion </gallery> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== [[Category:Chausuyama Kofun (Otsu)|qq=}} *[https://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3005.pdf Shiga Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
